title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Code Recall
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs fixtures, golden files, properties, fuzzing, html validation, and browser tests from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered parser-to-browser verification matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
Use small reviewed input, expected tree, diagnostics, route manifest, and output fixtures with explicit update review.
Check consumption, round-trip boundaries, tree validity, escaping, route uniqueness, determinism, clean equivalence, and idempotence across generated cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Test Pyramid · changed case · Review Test Pyramid
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 8254 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Validation Browser, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Validation Browser
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Validation Browser: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 4825 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine standards validation, internal-link and fragment checks, accessibility rules, keyboard tasks, multiple viewports, and representative published-host smoke tests.
